Red seaweeds (Rhodophyta) on Singapore shores: photo index

seaweeds text index | photo index Seaweeds

Division Rhodophyta Photo index of red seaweeds on Singapore shores
**Crunchy, hard

  • branching, encrusting**


Slender crunchy
pom-pom red seaweedJania sp.


Thick crunchy
pom-pom red seaweedFamily Galaxauraceae


Flat crunchy
pom-pom red seaweedAmphiroa sp.


Curled-tip
red seaweed Portieria hornemannii

3-6cm. Cluster of stiff, slender, cylindrical ‘stems’ regularly branched, tipped with a white rounded ‘cap’. Pink, maroon, purple. Bushy on rubble and ropes, sometimes a crunchy carpet among seagrasses. Commonly seen on all our shores. 3-6cm. Cluster of stiff, thick, cylindrical ‘stems’ regularly branched, a darker pink dot at the tip. Pink, maroon. Bushy among living reefs and on rubble. Commonly seen on our Southern shores. 5-8cm. Cluster of stiff, thick, cylindrical ‘stems’ forming Y-shapes, tipped with a white squarish ‘cap’. Pink. Flat layers (not bushy) among living reefs. Commonly seen on our Southern shores.   Short thin ‘stems’ finely branched with pointed projections giving an overall prickly appearance. On coral rubble. Sometimes seen on our Southern shores.


Encrusting
coralline algae awaiting identification.


Scaly red seaweed
Peyssonnelia sp.


Scaly red seaweed
Peyssonnelia sp.


Pimply
red seaweed Acrocystis nana

Thin, pink or red layer on hard surfaces. On coral rubble. Common on our Southern shores. 2-4cm circular disks, hard encrusting hard surfaces near reefs. Common on our Southern shores. 2-4cm circular disks, hard encrusting hard surfaces near reefs. Common on our Southern shores.   Clusters of tiny spherical or teardrop-shaped bumps, tightly packed together. On rocky shores. Sometimes seen on some of our shores.

seaweeds on this site

serrated strips
